01
Section 01

Brand Story

Domo-kun — NHK's beloved character since 1998 — is a creature unlike any other. Brown, rectangular, perpetually hungry, and endlessly lovable. Since first appearing as the mascot of NHK's BS broadcasting service, Domo-kun has captured hearts across Japan and around the world.

"A place where Domo-kun comes to life — warm, weird, and wonderful."

Domo Cafe brings that magic to life in Buena Park, California. Operated by GFC BP LLC under an official NHK license through Striker Entertainment, we are the first dedicated Domo-kun cafe experience in the United States.

Our mission is simple: create a space where the warmth of Japanese character culture meets the vibrant energy of Southern California. A place where families, fans, and foodies come together over beautifully crafted food and drinks — surrounded by the unmistakable presence of Domo-kun himself.

This isn't a branded pop-up. This is a destination. A genuine expression of what makes Domo-kun special — and a new home for fans in the United States.

Location
8340 La Palma Ave
Buena Park, CA 90620
www.domokuncafe.com
02
Section 02

The Character

Domo-kun is the intellectual property of NHK (Japan Broadcasting Corporation). All character usage must adhere strictly to NHK licensing guidelines, administered in the United States by Striker Entertainment.

Classic Domo

The iconic standard pose. Use for primary brand expressions, signage, and merchandise.

Domo Eating

Perfect for menu items, food photography context, and F&B promotional materials.

Domo Skate

Energetic, playful. Use for events, youth marketing, social media activations.

Character Usage Rules

  • Always use officially licensed NHK character assets — no fan art, no AI-generated versions
  • Never distort, skew, stretch, recolor, or alter Domo-kun in any way
  • Maintain minimum clear space: at least 1× the character height on all sides
  • Never place Domo-kun on busy, clashing, or non-approved background colors
  • Contact Striker Entertainment for any custom asset requests or new use cases
  • Character may not be used in conjunction with inappropriate content, alcohol (without approval), or competitive brands
  • All Domo-kun usage must be approved before going to print or publishing publicly
⚠️
Licensing Notice

Domo©NHK-TYO 1998-2026. Domo Animation©Domo Production Committee. All rights reserved. Licensed by Striker Entertainment. Unauthorized use of the Domo-kun character is strictly prohibited and may constitute trademark/copyright infringement.

03
Section 03

Logo System

The Domo Cafe logo is the primary visual identifier of our brand. It combines the warmth of the Domo-kun character with clean, modern typography to create a mark that is distinctive, memorable, and versatile across all applications.

Primary — Light Background

Preferred version. Use on white, cream, and warm light backgrounds.

Primary — Dark Background

Use on deep dark, charcoal, and dark photo backgrounds.

Chef Composite Logo

For food/beverage contexts: menus, packaging, staff badges, kitchen materials.

Chef Composite — Dark Background

Use for dark-themed menu inserts, blackboard signage, digital banners.

Clear Space

Always maintain a clear space equal to the height of the "D" in DOMO on all sides of the logo. Never crowd the logo with other elements.

Dashed border represents minimum clear space boundary — 1× logo height on all sides.

Minimum Size

  • Digital: 120px wide minimum
  • Print: 1 inch wide minimum
  • Favicon/App icon: use mark only (no wordmark)

Logo Don'ts

  • No rotation or skewing
  • No stretching or squishing
  • No recoloring or color alterations
  • No drop shadows, glows, or effects
  • No placement on busy or clashing backgrounds
  • No low-resolution usage (never pixelated)
04
Section 04

Color Palette

Our color palette is warm, grounded, and inviting — rooted in the natural tones of Domo-kun himself. Every color choice is intentional, reinforcing the cafe's welcoming character and Japanese-California identity.

Domo Brown
#8B4513
RGB 139, 69, 19
CMYK 0, 50, 86, 45
Primary brand color. Main identity, headers, key CTAs.
Cafe Red
#CC2222
RGB 204, 34, 34
CMYK 0, 83, 83, 20
Energy, appetite, Domo's mouth. Accent, highlights, CTAs.
Deep Dark
#1A0800
RGB 26, 8, 0
CMYK 0, 69, 100, 90
Headers, backgrounds, dark-mode base. Anchors the palette.
Warm White
#FDF8F4
RGB 253, 248, 244
CMYK 0, 2, 4, 1
Light mode base. Body text on dark. Never pure #FFFFFF.
Cream
#F5EDD8
RGB 245, 237, 216
CMYK 0, 3, 12, 4
Secondary background, card fills, menu backgrounds.
Tan
#D4A574
RGB 212, 165, 116
CMYK 0, 22, 45, 17
Secondary accent, link colors, warm highlights, borders.
Charcoal
#2C1A0E
RGB 44, 26, 14
CMYK 0, 41, 68, 83
Card backgrounds, secondary dark surfaces, nav elements.
Muted Brown
#9E7A60
RGB 158, 122, 96
CMYK 0, 23, 39, 38
Body text on dark, secondary labels, metadata, captions.
Do Not Use

Bright neons · Cool blues or teals · Greens · Purples · Any color that conflicts with the warm brown-red palette. When in doubt, ask.

05
Section 05

Typography

Our type system balances modern editorial confidence with warmth and approachability. Each font plays a specific role — use them consistently and don't substitute.

Display / Headings

Space Grotesk 700
32–80px
Letter-spacing: −0.02em
Line-height: 1.05–1.1

The warmth
of Domo.

Brand Labels

Outfit 600
10–14px
All caps
Letter-spacing: 0.2–0.3em

DOMO CAFE · BUENA PARK CA
NHK LICENSED · EST. 2026

Body Copy

DM Sans 300/400
14–16px
Line-height: 1.7–1.8
Max-width: 680px

Step inside and you'll find something different. Not just great food, but a whole world — Domo-kun's world. Every corner is designed to make you feel welcomed, surprised, and delighted.

Japanese Accent

Noto Serif JP 400/700
Sparingly only
For Japanese text
Never as substitute for other fonts

どうも。
ようこそ、どうもカフェへ。

Domo Display Font

Official NHK Brand Alphabet · A–Z · a–z · & . , · No Numbers
Use For
Signage & wayfinding · Merchandise text · Display headlines · Packaging · Feature graphics · In-store signs
Do Not Use For
Body copy · Menu descriptions · Legal text · Small sizes under 18pt · Numbers (font has no numerals)
⚠️ No Numbers: The Domo font does not include numerals (0–9). Use a separate compatible rounded sans-serif font for any pricing, dates, or numbered content.
Mono / Technical

JetBrains Mono 400/500
10–13px
For codes, labels, data
Never for body copy

#8B4513 → Domo Brown
#CC2222 → Cafe Red

06
Section 06

Voice & Tone

Domo Cafe's brand voice is distinct, consistent, and human. We sound like a place you want to spend time in — not a corporate brand, not a generic restaurant chain. Domo-kun sets the personality; we bring it to life in every word.

🌸 Warm

We greet you like family. Every message, every sign, every menu item — written as if a friendly host wrote it just for you. Never cold, never clinical.

😄 Playful

Domo-kun never takes himself too seriously. We don't either. Light wordplay, character-driven copy, and moments of genuine delight are all on-brand. Just don't force it.

✨ Genuine

No pretense, no filler, no corporate speak. We say what we mean. If the matcha latte is amazing, we say it's amazing — not "a curated beverage experience."

🎯 Crafted

Every detail is intentional. Our copy is considered, edited, and on-point. We take pride in our words just like we take pride in our food.

Words We Use & Avoid

✓ Words We Use
Warm Welcome Delight Discover Craft Genuine Together Cozy Invite Savor Create Share
✗ Words We Avoid
Cheap Fast Basic Generic Ordinary Value Discount Mass-market Convenient

Copy Examples

✓ We Say

  • "Come as you are. Domo's been waiting."
  • "Handcrafted with care, served with warmth."
  • "Your new favorite spot — Domo says so."
  • "Made fresh. Made with love. Made for you."
  • "Every visit is a little bit of Japan, right here."

✗ We Don't Say

  • "Best value in Buena Park!"
  • "A curated dining experience."
  • "Fast, fresh, and affordable."
  • "Premium quality at competitive prices."
  • "Industry-leading hospitality solutions."
07
Section 07

Photography & Visual Style

Our visual language is an extension of the brand's warmth. Every photo should feel like it belongs in the Domo Cafe universe — inviting, textured, character-driven, and distinctly not stock-photo.

🌅

Warmth First

Golden-hour tones. Amber light, soft shadows, rich warm gradients. Color-grade towards #D4A574 (tan) and #8B4513 (brown). Cool, clinical, or blue-tinted images are never on-brand.

🟫

Character-Led

When possible, integrate Domo-kun naturally into the scene — on shelves, by the table, in the background. The character should feel present, not forced. He lives here.

👥

Real People, Real Moments

Authentic guests enjoying the space. Candid over posed. Smiles that aren't performed. Community feel — diverse, warm, belonging. Never use obvious stock photography.

✓ Visual Do's

  • Warm color grading (golden, amber, brown tones)
  • Close-up food textures — steam, bubbles, garnishes
  • Natural light or warm artificial light sources
  • Domo-kun present in environment photos
  • Real guests — candid, joyful, authentic
  • Interior shots that show the cafe's character
  • Overhead (flat lay) food shots with warm surfaces

✗ Visual Don'ts

  • Cold white or blue-tinted backgrounds
  • Obvious stock photography or generic food images
  • Over-edited / HDR / hyper-saturated looks
  • Harsh shadows or unflattering lighting
  • Posed, stiff, or corporate-feeling subject shots
  • Any food shot that makes items look unappetizing
  • Competing brands or logos visible in frame
08
Section 08

Applications

The Domo Cafe brand lives across a wide range of touchpoints. Maintain consistency in color, typography, and character usage across every application — from the storefront to the social feed.

🏪

Exterior Signage

Primary logo centered, illuminated. Deep Dark background with Cafe Red and Warm White text. Domo-kun artwork flanking or integrated. Neon accent lighting in Cafe Red.

🪧

Interior Signage

Warm White backgrounds with Domo Brown headers. Mix of Outfit labels and Space Grotesk display copy. Character art throughout — walls, shelves, table tents.

📋

Menu Design

Chef composite logo on cover. Cream (#F5EDD8) background. DM Sans for descriptions. Outfit for item names (all caps, letter-spaced). Price in JetBrains Mono. Domo-kun character throughout.

👕

Staff Uniforms

Embroidered chef composite logo on chest. Domo Brown or Deep Dark base colors. Cafe Red accents. Clean, professional — Domo-kun should feel premium, not costume-y.

☕

Packaging

Cups: Domo Brown sleeve with chef logo in Warm White. Bags: Kraft with custom stamp in Cafe Red or Domo Brown. Boxes: clean Cream with Charcoal logo. Consistency across all formats.

📱

Social Media

Instagram/TikTok: warm food photography, character moments, behind-the-scenes. Template headers: Space Grotesk + Outfit label. Story formats: 9:16, Deep Dark with logo in corner.

🌐

Website

domokuncafe.com — dark mode default, full type system, character integration throughout. Hero: full-screen with Domo Cafe cover treatment. Menu CTA prominent. Google Maps integration.

📦

Merchandise

T-shirts, tote bags, plushies, mugs — all using licensed character assets, Domo Brown + Cafe Red palette, and either logo version. Submit all merch mockups to Striker Entertainment for approval.

🎪

Events & Pop-Ups

Consistent banner/backdrop system: Deep Dark bg, primary logo centered, character art. Brand-consistent tablecloths, standees, and collateral. Always carry brand guide for vendor reference.

09
Section 09

Brand Don'ts

These guidelines exist to protect the integrity of the brand, the NHK license, and the guest experience. When in doubt, refer to this guide or reach out to the brand team before proceeding.

✗

Off-Brand Colors

Never use neon colors, cool blues, teals, greens, or purples in any brand materials. Every element should stay within our warm palette.

✗

Alter Domo-kun

Never recolor, distort, stretch, AI-generate, or modify the Domo-kun character in any way. Only officially licensed NHK assets may be used.

✗

Non-Approved Fonts

Don't substitute fonts. If Space Grotesk doesn't load, use the fallback — never swap in Comic Sans, Impact, or random display fonts.

✗

Distort the Logo

Never stretch, rotate, squish, or add effects (shadows, glows, outlines) to the Domo Cafe logo. Always use the original file at proper proportions.

✗

Clashing Backgrounds

Don't place the logo or Domo-kun on busy patterns, low-contrast backgrounds, or colors that clash with the brand palette.

✗

Generic Photography

Don't use obvious stock photos, clipart, or generic food imagery. Domo Cafe visuals should always feel authentic, warm, and distinctly ours.

✗

Unauthorized Licensing

Never create merchandise, apparel, or printed materials using Domo-kun imagery without written approval from Striker Entertainment and NHK.

✗

Off-Voice Copy

Don't write in a corporate, cold, or generic tone. No buzzwords, no industry-speak. If it doesn't sound like something a warm, genuine host would say — rewrite it.

When in Doubt

Before creating any material that uses the Domo Cafe name, logo, or Domo-kun character, ask yourself: "Would this feel at home in a warm, crafted Japanese-California cafe?" If the answer is no — or if you're unsure — contact the brand team at izzy@domokuncafe.com before publishing.
